steps: Parse the description: payment \(p\) equals the original retail price minus $135 for each year since release. The original retail price is $670 and \(t\) is the number of years.
Spot-check with \(t = 1\): \(p = 670 - 135 = 535\). With \(t = 4\): \(p = 670 - 540 = 130\). Both are positive and sensible within the stated domain \(1 \leq t \leq 4\).

Evaluate every choice:
  • A: \(p = 135 - 670t\) — subtracts 670t, which would make \(p\) negative for any \(t \geq 1\). Wrong structure. Eliminated.
  • B: \(p = 670 - 135t\) — matches the derived equation exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: \(t = 135 - 670p\) — solves for \(t\), not \(p\), and uses impossible coefficients. Eliminated.
  • D: \(t = 670 - 135p\) — solves for \(t\) with swapped roles of the constants. Eliminated.
